title: Lively Designer
body: Developed a web-based kiosk orchestrating an end-to-end hardware loop. Managed MQTT-based environmental control for a 100" display, integrated iPad camera capture, and implemented IPP network printing with integrated queue management and hardware error-state handling.

title: Interactive Health
body: AdventHealth Interactive Health: Engineered a suite of zero-failure React kiosks for localized LAN deployment. Developed a matrix-driven scoring engine to categorize patient health personas, utilizing local MongoDB for data aggregation and Rive state machines for robust, high-performance animations across multiple hardware orientations.

title: ADV Flight 2030
body: Developed a high-pressure, one-week turnaround video sequencing engine for a multi-screen "Airplane Window" environment. Built a manifest-driven playback system with frame-accurate seeking to ensure each independent display was primed to sync with a live guided tour's narrative.

title: Halloween Horror Nights 2019 - 2022
body: Lead Interactive Engineer for a multi-year suite of in-app experiences. Architected a real-time AR zone-capture game utilizing GPS and MQTT for sub-second synchronization across thousands of concurrent users. Engineered narrative progression systems and QR-driven video modules with persistent state, while managing the Angular integration of custom high-performance components. Delivered a scalable, event-driven architecture using Node.js and Django capable of handling peak seasonal traffic.

title: NBC Studios – The Tour Show Control
body: Engineered a high-reliability Show Control suite for The Tour at NBC Studios. I architected a resilient, iPad-based interface utilizing MQTT and WebSockets to deliver sub-millisecond latency for live audio triggers and script timing during production. By migrating legacy workflows to a TypeScript-based architecture, I eliminated synchronization errors between production staff and technical assets, delivering a unified, reactive dashboard optimized for high-pressure, live-tour environments.

title: UniSign
body: Orchestrated the full-scale frontend rewrite of Universal’s internal NDA system from legacy Swift to Vue 3 (Options API) and strict TypeScript, subsequently expanding ownership to full-tech development. Currently manage the entire ecosystem, including UI/UX design and the Python (Django) / PostgreSQL backend. This end-to-end modernization streamlined the document-signing workflow for thousands of global enterprise users while maintaining 100% compliance with security standards.

title: Tour Site
body: Architected the custom digital hub for the Halo: Outpost Discovery touring fan experience. Built with a professional WordPress, Twig, and ACF tech, the platform managed dynamic body and engagement for thousands of attendees across the 2019 multi-city circuit. Archived previews available via Halopedia and Wayback Machine.

title: SATCOM App
body: Senior Systems Engineer and UI Lead overseeing the full three-generation evolution of satellite antenna control interfaces. I orchestrated a multi-year modernization strategy, transitioning legacy jQuery panels into a high-performance, component-based architecture. To support mission-critical remote deployments, I engineered Node.js gateways for Raspberry Pi hardware, ensuring low-latency hardware-to-UI synchronization and system uptime across every iteration of the platform.

title: Speakeasy Photobooth AI
body: Led the technical R&D and implementation of a generative AI-powered photobooth for Royal Caribbean. I successfully proposed a custom Stable Diffusion (A1111) filtering system, which expanded the project scope to include 1920s, 1950s, and 1980s styles. I architected a transition to ComfyUI to build a more robust, node-based workflow and oversaw the technical handoff to contractors, ensuring the final output maintained the original vision for high-fidelity, era-specific filtering.
